pattern: Recording the first index of each letter lets the second sighting verify the gap in one pass without nested scans.
hints: Each letter shows up exactly twice, so when you see a letter the second time you know both of its positions.
The gap requirement counts the letters strictly between the two occurrences, which is the index difference minus one.
Remember the first index of each character; on its second occurrence check that the index difference minus one equals the required distance for that letter.

edge_inputs.description: adjacent pair fails its required distance
adjacent pair with required distance zero passes
one letter off by one breaks the whole string
maximum gap between the only letter pair
letters far apart with non-zero gap

stress.description: random permutation of doubled letters with the matching distance array
stress.note: constructed to always be well-spaced; exercises the true branch on large valid inputs
